spec_match.rationale: Uses `counters(name, '.')` in both the visual and alt halves of the content property — exactly the spec change.

interactive.rationale: Live nested structure visible but interactive markers are minimal (2). Limited controls — visitor can't add/remove sections to see renumbering.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises multi-level numbering with both visual and alt sides; the page shows both.

edge_cases.rationale: Static nested tree; no demonstration of dynamic insertion/removal, deep nesting (4+ levels), or what happens when separator characters appear in section titles.

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5185442420621312 link in see-also.

openQuestions.title: Add add/remove section controls
openQuestions.detail: A button to add a sub-section dynamically would prove that the counters() values update in both visual and alt sides — currently it's a static tree.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate deep nesting
openQuestions.detail: Standards docs frequently nest 4-5 levels. Show that counters() handles arbitrary depth gracefully — and that the alt text remains comprehensible at depth 5.

summary: Good idea, light interactivity. The tree is static and needs dynamic insertion to be a stronger demo.
